title: pasta tests: automatically determine test parameters
body:
...from the test name. Eliminates scary duplication.

Followup to #19053: instead of cross-checking pasta test args
against test name, eliminate the args entirely. Determine
them all from the @test name itself.

Example:
```
   "TCP translated port range forwarding, IPv4, loopback"
     |  |          |    |     |           |     +-- iftype=loopback
     |  |          |    |     |           +-------- ip_ver=4
     |  |          |    |     +-------------------- bytes=1
     |  |          |    +-------------------------- range=3
     |  |          +------------------------------- (ignored)
     |  +------------------------------------------ delta=1
     +--------------------------------------------- proto=tcp
```
Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Oh, okay, now I get the purpose. But isn't it essentially the same as [what I proposed originally](https://github.com/containers/podman/compare/582a225617e187c5e6b39e2f4ec4f8bc08f5f86b..730818f7e7f6969424d6eed7bda2e82b934b77fb#diff-f79848cee954c128aa5f99629d792b654d9ee368590be1f2db0433f364e64a54)? Back then, you said we should avoid it because it's "action at distance" -- if the current duplication made you change your mind meanwhile, I would still prefer the original version because it avoided the `sed` thing.

For example, it's not clear to me why you have to drop `test_podman_networking_with_pasta-281-29_-2d_` from the test names. There must be some advantage with your intermediate name-mangling step but I can't quite grasp it...

Thank you for your feedback. I've [reworded and expanded two comments](https://github.com/containers/podman/compare/7145a0283b6ec254e80106a1b86d9d9b819815ac..4b1f40d38b4a028efa9e773d367ef407012649d8#diff-f79848cee954c128aa5f99629d792b654d9ee368590be1f2db0433f364e64a54), does that help? (TL;DR `sed` gives a human-readable string with non-information removed).

And, you are quite right to remind me of your initial version. I was wrong to dismiss that, and I'm sorry.

> Thank you for your feedback. I've [reworded and expanded two comments](https://github.com/containers/podman/compare/7145a0283b6ec254e80106a1b86d9d9b819815ac..4b1f40d38b4a028efa9e773d367ef407012649d8#diff-f79848cee954c128aa5f99629d792b654d9ee368590be1f2db0433f364e64a54), does that help? (TL;DR `sed` gives a human-readable string with non-information removed).

Sorry for being thick, but... in `-e 's/^test_podman_networking_with_pasta-281-29_-2d_//'`: why `281`, and why `29`? My original version was perhaps a bit oblivious, but it didn't _look_ like magic. :)

> And, you are quite right to remind me of your initial version. I was wrong to dismiss that, and I'm sorry.

No worries, I understand it looked somewhat unusual.

Oh! Yeah, `-` (dash) is BATS-ese for what the rest of the world recognizes as `%` or `#` instead:
```
-28 = %28 = chr(0x28) = '('
-29 =  ...            = ')'
-2D = ...             = '-'

 - with_pasta-281-29_-2d_xyz
 + with pasta(  1)   -   xyz      (extra spaces added for clarity)
```
That was the purpose behind this comment:
```bash
    # and convert punctuation (underscores and '-XX', which are escaped
    # ASCII codes, typically dashes and commas) to spaces. [...]
```
Suggestions _more_ than welcome on ways to make that clearer!

I guess marginally better:

`
    test_name_prefix="$(LC_TYPE=C printf "test_podman_networking_with_pasta%x1-%x_-%x_" "'('" "')" "'-'")"
`

...and use that for `sed`. And maybe, before saying that you strip and convert stuff, reporting an example of how the test "name" looks like at that point would be helpful (that's the main part I was missing).

If the goal is clarity for a maintenance programmer, how about [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/compare/4b1f40d38b4a028efa9e773d367ef407012649d8..427b959a1c7937c935781472f28922203e4681a9#diff-f79848cee954c128aa5f99629d792b654d9ee368590be1f2db0433f364e64a54)?

LGTM 
and happy green test buttons
